explanations.6: This downloads Quarto from GitHub in one long command; the backslash at the end means the command continues on the next line.
explanations.7: The tar command extracts the downloaded file into /opt/quarto, and --strip-components=1 removes the top folder level so files go directly where we want them.
explanations.8: This creates a shortcut (symlink) so you can type 'quarto' from anywhere instead of typing the full path /opt/quarto/bin/quarto.
explanations.13: This tells Python not to create compiled .pyc files, which saves space in the container since we don't need them.
explanations.19: The --no-cache-dir flag tells pip not to keep a copy of downloaded packages after installing, which shrinks the final image size.
